hero.title: H!NT Lab
hero.subtitle: Document Understanding · Human–Computer Interaction · Collaborative Cognition

labIntro.title: H!NT Lab
labIntro.p1: H!NT Lab (Human-Intelligence iNTegration Lab) explores how human intelligence and machine intelligence can work together more deeply. Our goal is to build human–AI collaboration systems that are more natural, efficient, and genuinely useful.
labIntro.researchIntro: Our research is organized around three closely connected areas:

areas.title: Document Understanding
areas.description: Multimodal document understanding, generation, and intelligent analysis

areas.title: Human–Computer Interaction
areas.description: Natural, efficient interaction technologies and human–AI collaboration systems

areas.title: Collaborative Cognition
areas.description: How human cognition and AI can complement each other in complex tasks

labIntro.synergy: These three directions reinforce one another. Document understanding provides the foundation for information processing; interaction technologies make communication between humans and AI smoother; and collaborative cognition helps us rethink how human–AI systems can become more effective as a whole.

about.title: About
about.p1: Dr. Hao Wang served as a Lecturer in the Department of Intelligence Science and Technology at Shanghai University from 2019 to 2024, and has been an Associate Professor since 2024. He is currently seconded to the Putuo District Data Bureau / Administrative Approval Service Center in Shanghai, where he serves as Deputy Director. He received a doctoral fellowship from the China Scholarship Council and earned his M.Sc. (2014) and Ph.D. (2019) from Waseda University, Japan. He has worked at Microsoft Research Asia, Toshiba Global R&D, and NTT Communication Science Laboratories. He has published more than 40 papers in international conferences and journals, including ACL, EMNLP, COLING, and CogSci. More than 30 of these papers have appeared in SCI/SSCI/A&HCI-indexed venues or CCF-recommended conferences. He has led a completed Shanghai Municipal Science and Technology Commission Sailing Program project, an NSFC Young Scientists Fund project at the AI interdisciplinary interface, and five industry collaboration projects. As a key member, he has also contributed to several NSFC major and general projects, the National Key R&D Program, and special projects on social governance. He has served as a long-term PC member and reviewer for leading AI and NLP venues, including ICLR, ICML, NeurIPS, ACL, EMNLP, and AAAI, as well as journals such as TKDD, TNNLS, and NLE. His research interests include artificial intelligence, human–AI collaborative cognition, and natural language processing, with applications in medical and other high-stakes documents.
about.p2: We welcome students with backgrounds in NLP, as well as students from linguistics, cognitive science, psychology, and related fields. International students are also welcome. Please note that Shanghai University currently offers Chinese-taught programs only, so international applicants are expected to hold an HSK Level 5 certificate.

projects.title: Research Projects
projects.subtitle: Selected projects currently underway in our lab

cards.name: Deepling.tech
cards.description: An AI-powered technology commentary platform with a distinct voice, known for sharp, witty, and fully automated tech reviews.

cards.name: DeepTrans Studio
cards.description: A professional AI-assisted translation platform for high-quality multilingual document translation and post-editing.

cards.name: DeepMed Search
cards.description: An intelligent medical literature retrieval system that uses deep learning and knowledge graphs to support precise search and recommendation for medical researchers.

cards.name: ChineseLawTranslation
cards.description: A Chinese–English legal translation project that builds high-quality legal terminology and translation resources to support the international communication of Chinese law.

expectations.title: To Prospective Students
paragraphs: Over the past year, I made 870 commits on GitHub.

At H!NT Lab, we care less about titles or polished resumes, and more about whether people are willing to do real work. Research and engineering are not built by talk alone. They take code, debugging, trial and error, and steady progress over time.

I do not judge students only by their background, major, or prior experience. You may have a weak foundation, come from a non-CS background, or have little experience at the beginning. What matters more to me is whether you are willing to learn, try things yourself, and take one concrete step forward when facing a real problem.

Your first code may be rough. Your first idea may be naive. That is fine. What I value is whether you have actually tried, whether you can explain what you did, where you got stuck, and how you plan to improve.
If you would like to contact me, please first read one of the projects on this page and briefly answer the following three questions in your email:
Which problem or direction interests you?
What do you think this project is trying to solve?
If you were to get involved, what small step would you try first? This could be a piece of code, a simple experiment, a literature review, or a concrete analysis.
There is no need for a long self-introduction or a generic statement of interest. I would rather see a small sign of real thinking and action.
Your starting point matters less than your willingness to act. I look forward to working with students who are ready to learn by doing and to build something meaningful together.

insights.title: Insights
quoteLines: Rigorous research, grounded in real problems and shaped by human–AI collaboration.
Seek truth, stay grounded, and move forward one step at a time.
